_meta.description: 12-week golden ACWR fixture for Program Engine load-math regression tests.
_meta.primary_source: pitcher_program_app/docs/superpowers/research/2026-05-31-program-engine-recon.md Front 5 — transcribed verbatim during recon agent execution on a machine that could resolve the Google Drive aliases.
_meta.original_xlsx: Ramp up with Bullpen ramp up  (1).xlsx (12 weeks); present in pitcher_program_app/past_arm_programs/ only as a 1108-byte Drive alias on this host (openpyxl: 'File is not a zip file').
_meta.load_units_formula_hint: G ≈ throws × intensity_factor × distance_factor. Empirical anchor: G/(dist × throws) = 0.894 at (45ft, 50%) ⇒ when calibrated to (1.0 × pct_decimal × dist_factor) suggests dist_factor for 45ft ≈ 1.79 (or pct-decimal × distance_in_ft normalization). Phase 2.1 load_math calibrates against this single anchor + the weekly curve.
_meta.deload_pattern: 3-up-1-down undulation. Wk4 dips below Wk3 (10375 < 10935). Wk7 dips below Wk6 (12090 < 13516). The empty %increase / ACWLR columns on the original xlsx are the human ACWR governor mental model — Phase 2 implements them deterministically.
use_in_tests: tests/test_golden_acwr_curve.py — locks the curve + invariants here.
tests/test_load_math.py (Phase 2.1) — recomputes weekly G from daily 5-tuples once a full daily fixture is available; asserts 5% tolerance vs this curve.
guardrail #1 ACWR band check (Phase 2.4) — uses this curve as a known-good fixture that must validate as 'valid'.
gaps: Daily 5-tuple grid for all 12 × ~5 days is NOT in this fixture — only the verified anchor above. The Drive-aliased xlsx is the missing source. If/when the operator resolves the alias (Finder → 'Show Original' → save outside Drive) into data/knowledge/golden/ramp_up_with_bullpen_12wk.xlsx, scripts/extract_golden_acwr_curve.py (Phase 0.3 follow-up) can extract the full daily grid and replace this fixture in place.

weekly_G_note: Indices 0-11 correspond to weeks 1-12. Wks 10-11 (14000, 14300) are interpolated linearly between recon's '…' range (week 9: 13620, week 12: 14616) since only the endpoint of the missing-middle was transcribed. Replace with real values once the xlsx is recoverable.
